title: Fenn Adeyemi — Senior Product Manager, Anchor Codec
compiled_truth:
Fenn Adeyemi is a Senior Product Manager at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), where he owns the product surface area spanning developer experience, API design, and integration workflows. Fenn joined in August 2026 after spending nearly four years leading product for data pipelines and observability tooling at a Series B SaaS company — experience that gave him an unusually deep intuition for how infrastructure teams actually evaluate and adopt new primitives. He's known internally for being product-obsessed in a way that sometimes borders on compulsive: he'll rewrite spec documents three or four times before sharing them, not out of insecurity but because he genuinely belives the spec is the product at the earliest stages.

Collaboratively, Fenn is one of those PMs who engineers actually like working with. He doesn't treat eng as an execution layer; he pulls engineers into discovery calls, shares raw customer transcripts, and runs lightweight design sprints that feel more like jam sessions than formal ceremonies. This approach has made him a natural bridge between the founding engineering team and the growing go-to-market org at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor). Several teammates have noted that Fenn's presence in a meeting tends to reduce the number of follow-up meetings needed — he synthesizes fast and biases toward written decisions.

Before his SaaS stint, Fenn studied computer science and cognitive science at university, which shows up in his thinking about developer ergonomics. He's particularly focused on reducing what he calls "time-to-first-correct-integration" — the window between a developer reading docs and successfully moving real data through Anchor's single-pass primitives. This metric has become something of a north star for the product org under his influence.

Fenn tends to be quiet in large group settings but sharp in small ones. He writes long internal memos, sometimes to a fault. His Slack presence is steady but not overwhelming — he prefers threads to channels, docs to messages. Outside of work he's an amateur distance runner and occasionally posts race photos that nobody asked for. One or two people have joked that his pacing strategy mirrors his product style: disciplined, patient, then a hard push at the end.
timeline:
- **2026-08-15** | Fenn Adeyemi joins [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as Senior Product Manager, initially focused on developer onboarding flows.
- **2026-09-22** | Publishes first internal product memo, "Integration Friction Audit," cataloging 14 pain points reported by early design partners.
- **2026-11-03** | Leads a cross-functional sprint to redesign the SDK quickstart experience; ships revised docs and sample apps within two weeks.
- **2027-01-18** | Introduces "time-to-first-correct-integration" as a tracked product metric; begins instrumenting onboarding funnels.
- **2027-04-09** | Runs a series of customer discovery interviews with pipeline engineering teams at three mid-market prospects, feeding insights back to the platform team.
- **2027-07-26** | Presents internally on competitive positioning, arguing Anchor's single-pass approach changes the buying conversation for infrastructure buyers.
- **2027-10-11** | Co-authors a public blog post on deterministic data infrastructure adoption patterns; post gets moderate traction in the dev community.
- **2028-01-14** | Takes on expanded scope covering the enterprise integration tier, working closely with solutions engineering.
- **2028-05-02** | Facilitates an offsite product planning session for H2 2028 roadmap; credited with aligning eng and GTM on three priority bets.

_facts.name: Fenn Adeyemi
_facts.title: Senior Product Manager
_facts.background: Led product for data pipelines and observability tools at a Series B SaaS company
